suites.conclusion: Custom KDTree demonstrates pruning logic; SciPy/sklearn are expected to win raw throughput at large N, while voxel hash is competitive for fixed-radius locality.
Point-to-point ICP is accurate near the solution but remains sensitive to initialization, noise, and outliers.
RANSAC remains stable with moderate outliers until clean minimal samples become unlikely.
Feature-based global registration expands ICP's basin of convergence by estimating a coarse pose first.
Euclidean clustering runtime scales with radius-neighborhood queries and benefits directly from spatial indexing.
